fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Mississippi Pharmacy Technician License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a pharmacy technician? Here's whether your license transfers to Mississippi - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: pharmacy technician license reciprocity mississippi, how to transfer pharmacy technician license to mississippi, pharmacy technician compact mississippi, mississippi pharmacy technician endorsement application

board.name: Mississippi Board of Pharmacy
board.mailing_address: 6512 Old Canton Road, Suite 200, Jackson, MS 39211

license_types.title: Certified Pharmacy Technician (CPhT)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a pharmacy technician in Mississippi.

steps.title: 1. Create an Account and Start Online Application
steps.description: Visit the Mississippi Board of Pharmacy Online Licensing Portal (portal.mbop.ms.gov). If you are a new user, click 'Register' to create an account. Once registered and logged in, locate and select the 'Pharmacy Technician Initial Application' to begin the online application process.

steps.title: 2. Submit Required Documentation Electronically
steps.description: As part of your online application, upload a clear, current, color photograph suitable for identification. Also, submit proof of high school graduation or its equivalent (e.g., GED) and proof of certification from a Board-approved national certification body (PTCB or NHA).

steps.title: 3. Complete Criminal History Background Check
steps.description: Obtain a criminal history background check by submitting fingerprints through a state-approved vendor, IdentoGO. Follow the instructions provided on the Board's website regarding the fingerprinting process and required codes for Mississippi Board of Pharmacy applicants.

steps.title: 4. Pay the Application Fee
steps.description: Pay the non-refundable application fee of $100.00 directly through the Mississippi Board of Pharmacy Online Licensing Portal as part of your application submission. Fees are subject to change, so verify the current amount on the Board's fee schedule page.

steps.title: 5. Await Board Review and Licensure
steps.description: After submitting all required documentation, background check results, and fees, the Board will review your application. You may be eligible to work for up to 90 days from the date of initial application submission pending final licensure, provided all items EXCEPT national certification have been submitted.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam.

provisional_text: Mississippi offers a temporary or trainee pharmacy technician permit that allows new applicants to begin working in a pharmacy setting under direct pharmacist supervision while completing their certification requirements. Contact the Mississippi Board of Pharmacy for specific temporary permit restrictions and duration.
reciprocity_text: Pharmacy technicians registered in another state may apply for registration in Mississippi by submitting a new application to the Mississippi Board of Pharmacy. There is no interstate compact for pharmacy technicians. Applicants must provide proof of current national certification (PTCB or ExCPT), complete any Mississippi-specific requirements, and pass a criminal background check. Processing typically takes 2 Weeks.

faqs.question: How much does a Mississippi pharmacy technician license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Mississippi have reciprocity for pharmacy technicians?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers pharmacy technician practice in Mississippi. To work in Mississippi, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my pharmacy technician license to Mississippi?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Mississippi while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Pharmacy technicians registered in another state may apply for registration in Mississippi by submitting a new application to the Mississippi Board of Pharmacy. There is no interstate compact for pharmacy technicians. Applicants must provide proof of current national certification (PTCB or ExCPT), complete any Mississippi-specific requirements, and pass a criminal background check. Processing typically takes 2 Weeks.

hero.title: Become a Pharmacy Technician in Mississippi
hero.dek: Apply online through the Mississippi Board of Pharmacy Online Licensing Portal (portal.mbop.ms.gov). Create an account, select the Pharmacy Technician application, and follow the instructions to submit your application and supporting documents.
